item.title: How AI Is Creeping Into ‘The New York Times’
item.description: Artificial intelligence seems to be turning up, undisclosed, in the opinion pages of major news publications.
item.dc:creator: Vauhini Vara

item.title: Twenty Seconds of ‘Task Saturation’ at LaGuardia
item.description: Having two controllers on a midnight shift might be standard procedure, but they can still be overwhelmed.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Mondor

item.title: How To Guess If Your Job Will Exist in Five Years
item.description: Ask yourself: Are you coal, or are you a horse?
item.dc:creator: Annie Lowrey

item.title: The Deep Risk That Republican Hawks Overlooked
item.description: If the Iran war goes badly, the isolationist, anti-Israel wing of the party is likely to steer the GOP’s future.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Chait

item.title: Where Are All the Campus Protests?
item.description: Two years ago, students occupied buildings and colonized the quad. Now the same places are strangely silent.
item.dc:creator: Rose Horowitch

item.title: America Needs a Trimmer
item.description: Some people long for bold, visionary leaders. Others value technocratic expertise. But there’s a third alternative.
item.dc:creator: David Brooks

item.title: What the Markets Tell Trump
item.description: The war in Iran is a reminder that market movements can play an important role in the president’s decision making.
item.dc:creator: Will Gottsegen

item.title: Why Does Watching TV Feel Like Homework? (Just Me?)
item.description: Binge-watching has become a way of life, for better or worse.
item.dc:creator: Shirley Li

item.title: Today’sAtlanticTrivia: Ill-Fated Francophilia
item.description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
item.dc:creator: Drew Goins

item.title: Military Families Once Again Brace for a Knock
item.description: For the first time in years, the U.S. is losing service members to a foreign war.
item.dc:creator: Marie-Rose Sheinerman

item.title: ‘You Want to Leave Us Alone With Mojtaba?’
item.description: I’ve spoken with more than a dozen Iranians since the start of the war. Most are terrified, and some are losing faith in America.
item.dc:creator: Arash Azizi

item.title: The Most Urgent Issue for the U.S. Catholic Church Isn’t Abortion Anymore
item.description: Trump’s second term has transformed the priorities of the American Catholic hierarchy.
item.dc:creator: Francis X. Rocca

item.title: Canada’s Polite Pogrom
item.description: Is a national tolerance for zealotry purging Jews from public life?
item.dc:creator: Jesse Brown

item.title: The WNBA Players Got What They Wanted
item.description: A new labor agreement represents a breakthrough in women’s-sports history.
item.dc:creator: Jemele Hill

item.title: The Meme-Washing of RFK Jr.
item.description: The online persona of America’s health secretary is very different from the man himself.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Florko

item.title: The End of Human Rights
item.description: “If there is an office called ‘Human Rights,’ it seems its employees have closed their eyes and ears.”
item.dc:creator: George Packer

item.title: Is Trump Actually Having ‘Very Good’ Talks With Tehran?
item.description: The disconnect between the president’s claims and Iran’s denials underscores how little control either side has over the conflict—or its narrative.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Salama

item.title: Iran Is Trying to Defeat America in the Living Room
item.description: The regime knows that its best ally against American power is American public opinion.
item.dc:creator: Karim Sadjadpour

item.title: There Were Warnings
item.description: Americans are learning that public safety is not a given.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Kayyem

item.title: Europe’s Far Right Is Turning on Trump
item.description: The president’s attempt to influence elections across the Atlantic is backfiring.
item.dc:creator: Isaac Stanley-Becker
